description: Based on the specialty of the instructor, selected well-known machine learning algorithms will be studied in great mathematical detail.  General topics may include Regression, Dimension Reduction, Density Estimations, and Classification. Advanced topics may include Google PageRank algorithm, Manifold Learning, and the Mapper algorithm. Prerequisites: MAT 653 .

title: Advanced Topics in Mathematics
description: Topics vary according to interest and demand, and include algebra, applied mathematics, combinatorics, dynamics, mathematical logic, topology, and other topics. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic varies.

title: Financial Mathematics for Actuaries
description: Measurement of interest, present and accumulated value, amortization, sinking funds, bonds, duration, immunization, and an introductory analysis of financial derivatives. Intended to help prepare for the FM/2 actuarial exam. Prerequisites: Minimum grade of C (2.0) in MAT 394 or permission of instructor. Graduate students in MA in Mathematics, Ph.D. students in Computational Mathematics, or undergraduate students in the accelerated BS to MA in Mathematics.

title: Project in Mathematics
description: Directed research project in Mathematics. Prerequisites: Admission to the MA in Mathematics and Permission of Instructor.

title: Abstract Algebra I
description: The first half of a year-long sequence in algebra.  Topics include quotients and products of groups, the Sylow theorems, and structure theory for finitely generated abelian groups, representations of groups, and the basic theory of rings.

title: Abstract Algebra II
description: The second half of a year-long sequence in algebra. Topics include integral domains, ideals, factorization, polynomial rings, quotient rings, finite fields, field extensions, and Galois theory. Prerequisites: MAT 691 or permission of instructor.

title: Mathematical Analysis
description: Real number axioms, metric spaces, sequences, series, continuity, differentiation, the Riemann-Stieltjes integral.

title: Mathematical Analysis
description: Real number axioms, metric spaces, sequences, series, continuity, differentiation, the Riemann-Stieltjes integral.

title: Thesis  1-6
description: Individual guidance in the development and examination of a research problem.

title: Graduate Seminar in Computational Mathematics
description: Readings from the literature of computational mathematics. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor.

title: Topics in Computational Mathematics
description: Advanced study in special topics in computational mathematics. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor.

title: Mathematical Cryptography
description: Mathematics of cryptography with emphasis on public key systems. Applications of elliptic and hyperelliptic curves and lattice theory in attacking and evaluating the security of cryptographic systems. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor.

title: Numerical Mathematics
description: Functional analytic treatment of computation, approximation, optimization, interpolation, smoothing equations, linear systems, differential equations. Emphasis on the mathematical development and analysis of numerical techniques.

title: Finite Element Methods
description: Introduce the fundamental concepts of the finite element method for approximating solutions to boundary and initial boundary value problems. Topics include modeling, mathematical formulations, convergence analysis, and computer implementation. Prerequisites: A grade of B or better in MAT 727 .

title: Linear Algebra
description: An introduction to vector spaces over general fields.  Topics include linear transformations, dual spaces, inner product spaces, orthogonality, spectral theory, and Jordan normal form.

title: Numerical Linear Algebra
description: An introduction to numerical linear algebra.  Topics include conditioning and stability, matrix factorizations, eigenvalue solvers, least-squares problems, and iterative methods as well as the implementation of various computational methods using a high-level programming language.

title: Ordinary Differential Equations
description: Existence and uniqueness theorems for initial value problems, theory of linear equations, nonlinear equations, stability theory, boundary value problems.

title: Partial Differential Equations
description: Derivation of partial differential equations (PDE) models and applications, linear first order PDE's, elliptic equations and Green's function, PDE's of parabolic and hyperbolic type. Prerequisites: MAT 735 or permission of instructor.

title: General Topology
description: Topological spaces, point set topology, product and quotient spaces, embedding and metrization, uniform spaces, function spaces, homotopy theory, simplicial complexes and homology, more algebraic topology, general homology theories.

title: Algebra I: Groups and Rings
description: The first half of a year-long sequence in algebra.  Topics include basic group and ring theory:  quotients and products of groups, group actions, the Sylow theorems, structure theory for finitely generated abelian groups, domains, ideals, factorization, and polynomial rings.

title: Algebra II: Modules and Fields
description: The second half of a year-long sequence in algebra.  Topics include basic module and field theory:  quotient modules, tensor products, exact sequences, structure theory for finitely generated modules over a PID, extension of scalars, finite fields, algebraic field extensions, and Galois theory. Prerequisites: MAT 740 or permission of instructor.

title: Computational Algebraic Number Theory
description: A constructive introduction to algebraic number theory.  Topics include algebraic number fields,  integral bases, ideal factorization, system of fundamental units, and the class group. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor.

title: Complex Analysis
description: The complex number system, holomorphic functions, power series, complex integration, representation theorems, the calculus of residues.

title: Measure Theory
description: Lebesgue measure, and integration; existence of non-measurable sets; sigma-algebras; transfinite induction; abstract measure spaces, measurable functions, and integration; outer measures and Caratheodory's Extension Theorem; product measures; Fubini's Theorem; Egorov's Theorem; Fatou's Lemma; Dominated Convergence Theorem; modes of convergence, Borel-Cantelli Lemma.

title: Real Analysis
description: Lebesque measure; the Lebesque integral; differentiation and integration, the classical Banach spaces; metric spaces, topological spaces, compact spaces; Banach spaces, measure and integration, measure and outer measure; the Daniell integral; mappings of measure spaces. Prerequisites: Bachelor's degree with a major in mathematics. Credits equivalent to credits for mathematics MAT 310 , MAT 311 , MAT 595, and MAT 596, or permission of instructor and department head.

title: Computational Topology
description: Triangulations and WRAP. Computing homology algorithmically. Morse theory and persistent homology. Computations on knots, braids, and links. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor.

title: Computational Algebra
description: An introduction to basic subjects in computational algebra. Topics include fast arithmetic, algorithms for finite fields, matrix normal forms over rings, polynomial factorization, and lattice basis reduction.

title: The Mathematics of Machine Learning
description: This course covers the underlying mathematical theory behind common machine learning methods: support vector machines, kernel methods, decision trees, nearest neighbor, clustering, stochastic gradient descent, and neural networks.

title: Advanced Topological Data Analysis
description: This is an advanced course in topological data analysis. After covering persistent homology, bar codes, and stability, the course will move into research level topics drawn from recent papers.

title: Directed Doctoral Research   1-6
description: Individual work on a dissertation research problem, which could also include original research or a review of current literature leading to a dissertation proposal. Prerequisites: Permission of Director of Graduate Study.

title: Thesis Extension  1-3
description: Thesis Extension.

title: Dissertation Extension  1-3
description: Dissertation Extension.

title: Understanding Media
description: Introduction to the discipline of Media Studies with emphasis on industry development, delivery systems, and audiences in a changing media landscape.

title: WUAG Workshop
description: Supervised participation in radio broadcasting or program production at the campus radio station WUAG. Notes: May be repeated for credit for a maximum of 3 s.h.

title: Media Writing
description: Introduction to the theory and practice of media writing, as well as the demands and characteristics of various media forms and formats. Includes concentrated exercises in developing, shaping, and scripting messages. CIC: CIC College Writing

title: Media Literacy
description: Introduction to the critical analysis of media, its impacts, and its methods.

title: Film Appreciation
description: Analysis of selected, significant motion pictures of the world's cinema, from the silent period to the present. MAC: MAC CritThink Hum and Fine Art

title: Television Appreciation
description: Analysis of the cultural and artistic significance of selected television programs. MAC: MAC CritThink Hum and Fine Art

title: Introduction to Media Production for Non-majors
description: Introduction to the basic aesthetic and technical concepts in media production and postproduction editing. Prerequisites: Non-Media Studies majors.

title: Introduction to Media Production
description: Introduction to techniques of media production with emphasis on video capture and editing. Prerequisites: Media Studies major or minor.

title: Media Theory
description: Examination of media texts, contexts, influence, and effects from a variety of theoretical perspectives. Prerequisites: MST 100 .

title: Introduction to Film History
description: Introduction to the study of international film history from the late 19th century to the present. Prerequisites: MST 100 .

title: History of Digital Media
description: Emergence, structure, and history of radio, television, digital, and web-based media, as well as their practices and impacts on individuals and society. Prerequisites: MST 100 .

title: Emerging Technologies
description: A survey of alternative forms of storytelling. Looking at the new frontiers and narrative strategies, including immersive video, interactivity, installation, performance technologies, collective storytelling, transmedia, mobile platform strategies, and beyond.

title: Topics in Media
description: Focusing on variable topics; examines film as a reflection of and expression of culture. Prerequisites: MST 100 . Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic changes.

title: Gender and Media Culture
description: Examination of the nature of media contents and production processes as they influence the construction of feminine, masculine, and non-binary identities. CIC: CIC College Writing

title: News Analysis
description: Analysis of news practices and presentation across multiple media and formats from a variety of theoretical, philosophical, and historical perspectives.

title: Topics in Non-Western Media
description: Study of films from Latin America, Africa, the Middle East, or Asia. Prerequisites: MST 100 . Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic changes.

title: Cult Films
description: Explores cultural contexts and technological developments that help form small but influential cults around certain films. Students will learn how taboo-breaking fringe filmmaking impacts mainstream cinema and culture.

title: Broadcast Newswriting
description: Writing and planning newscasts for broadcast media. CIC: CIC College Writing Prerequisites: Media Studies major or minor. or permission of instructor.

title: Creative Copywriting
description: Analysis of persuasive media and examination of radio, television, and Internet advertising techniques with emphasis on developing writing skills for electronic media advertising and promotional campaigns. CIC: CIC College Writing

title: Writing for the Screen
description: Study and practice of script writing resulting in an original short script. CIC: CIC College Writing Prerequisites: Media Studies major or minor. or permission of instructor.

title: Creative Process in Film and Video
description: Various approaches for the enhancement of the visual imagination, emphasizing the origination of ideas and their development into treatments and proposals for film and video.

title: Announcing for Media
description: Theory and practice of announcing skills and techniques in radio, television, and digital media. Prerequisites: Media Studies major or Radio minor or permission of instructor.

title: Fiction Film Production
description: Introduction to fiction filmmaking, using moving images and sound to convey stories and ideas. Emphasis is given to narrative conceptualization and technical skills. Prerequisites: MST 271 or permission of instructor.

title: Sound
description: Techniques and aesthetics of digital sound design for the moving image. Prerequisites: MST 271 or permission of instructor.

title: Lighting
description: Principles of light and color in lighting for television and film production. Application of the equipment and accessories used in the execution of lighting design through practical projects. Prerequisites: MST 271 .

title: Cinematography
description: Principles and practice of cinematography. Application of the equipment and accessories used in cinematography through practical projects. Prerequisites: MST 271 .

title: Editing I
description: Development of practical editing skills in pacing, shot selection, and the workflow necessary for delivery. Prerequisites: MST 271 . Media Studies major or minor; or permission of instructor.

title: Virtual Production
description: Application of principles and techniques of virtual production, including real-time workflows integrating physical and digital production environments. Prerequisites: MST 271 or permission of instructor.

title: Gaming and Esports Production
description: This course integrates the application of video production techniques and live streaming to gaming and esports media. In addition, the course critically analyzes the conceptual frames and themes of esports media “texts” as well as how they impact and are impacted by beliefs about gender, race, and culture. Prerequisites: MST 271 or Permission of Instructor.

title: Special Problems  1-3
description: Guided individual study in an area of special interest to the student. Prerequisites: Permission of faculty supervisor is required prior to registration. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Spartan Sportslink Practicum
description: Supervised participation in the live video production of UNCG Athletics events. Notes: May be repeated once for credit.

title: Independent Study
description: Guided practice in a creative area of media. Prerequisites: Permission of faculty supervisor required prior to registration. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Emerging Technologies Studio
description: A studio course where students will work with newer technologies to experiment with alternative forms of image-making and storytelling bridging technical innovation and experimentation with socially engaged content. Prerequisites: MST 314 .

title: Media Law and Ethics
description: Study of media law and questions of ethics as they apply to the mass media.

title: Film Theory
description: Study of the principal theories of film through the writings of critics, theorists, and directors. Prerequisites: MST 225 or permission of instructor.

title: Media Financing and Distribution
description: The processes of raising and budgeting funds and distributing film and videos for theatrical release, direct DVD/video release, or television broadcast.

title: Topics in Film Studies
description: Study of selected topics in fictive or nonfictive films, including genres, works of an individual director, or other emphasis. Prerequisites: MST 302 . Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic changes.

title: Topics in Digital Media
description: Study of selected topics in fictive or non-fictive programming for digital media including genres, delivery systems, or other emphasis. Prerequisites: MST 303 . Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic changes.

title: Topics in Media Writing
description: Study and practice of selected topics in media writing, which may include journalism, public relations, advertising, digital content, or writing for film and television. Emphasis on writing for specific audiences and purposes within ethical and professional standards. CIC: CIC College Writing Prerequisites: Media Studies major or minor. or permission of instructor; Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic varies.

title: Doing Visual History
description: Explores the interstices of history, documentary production, and personal narratives.

title: Seminar in Media Studies
description: This seminar class aims to bring together knowledge gained from various media studies courses to develop a cohesive understanding of the field.  It delves into a focused area within media, requiring original research and critical analysis of media texts and their impact. Prerequisites: MST 302 or MST 303 , 9 credits from the following: MST 301 , MST 321 , MST 325 , MST 330 , MST 407 , MST 425, MST 427 , MST 428 , MST 453 , MST 459 or permission from the instructor.

title: Unreal Engine for Filmmaking
description: Advanced study of Unreal Engine as a filmmaking tool, with emphasis on cinematics, environment design, and virtual production workflows. Prerequisites: MST 378 or permission of instructor.

title: Editing II
description: Survey of the history, aesthetics, and techniques in sequencing moving images. Prerequisites: MST 375 or permission of instructor.

title: Virtual Production II
description: Advanced study of virtual production with emphasis on collaborative projects, experimental techniques, and integration of emerging technologies. Prerequisites: MST 378 and MST 465 . or permission of instructor.

title: Directing
description: Fundamentals of screen directing, which includes directing actors, script breakdown, scene blocking, communication with cast and crew, and the logistics of production. Prerequisites: MST 271 .

title: Topics in Media Production
description: Advanced application of principles and techniques of the selected media production. Prerequisites: MST 271 . Media Studies majors or minors; Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic varies.

title: Professional Practice
description: Exploration of professional capabilities applied to media production and studies for upcoming graduating Media Studies majors. It is designed to equip students with the essential knowledge and skills needed for success in their chosen field. It aims to bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world application. Prerequisites: Media Studies major. Senior standing; or permission of instructor.

title: Client Based Production
description: Collaborative work on the production of a client sponsored program. Emphasis on collaboration in a timely, professional manner in an environment in which students may not have complete creative control. Prerequisites: MST 271 and MST 375 . or permission of instructor; Notes: May be repeated for a total of 6 s.h.

title: Animation Production
description: Study and practice of animation techniques. Prerequisites: MST 271 and MST 375 , or permission of instructor.

title: Documentary Production
description: Documentary construction, research, planning, and production techniques. Further development of video production skills in a supervised project. Prerequisites: MST 271 and MST 375 . or permission of instructor.

title: Media Thesis Production
description: This capstone course is designed to bring together ideas, processes, practices, and theories in the service of the production of a substantial work of cinema. Students will pursue an entire project from conception to completion, combining intensive preproduction, production, and post-production with in-depth instruction on lab work, distribution, and exhibition. Prerequisites: Media Studies major. Senior standing; or permission of instructor.

title: Advanced WUAG Workshop
description: Supervised participation at an advanced level of radio broadcasting or program production at the campus radio station WUAG. Notes: May be repeated for credit for a maximum of 3 s.h.

title: Media Internship  1-6
description: Field learning experience in media industries. Academic supervision provided by faculty member and direction in the field provided by job supervisor. Prerequisites: Restricted to Media Studies majors and permission of the Director of Internships. Notes: May be repeated for credit for total of 6 s.h.

title: Advanced Media Production Practicum  1-3
description: Advanced, in-depth, hands-on exposure to the many aspects of the production and postproduction process of film and electronic visualization. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. Notes: May be repeated for a total of three (3) semester hours.

title: History of Film to
description: Advanced study of world cinema from its prehistory in the late 19th century to the beginning of World War II in Europe, emphasizing significant movements, genres, and filmmakers.

title: History of Film since
description: Advanced study of world cinema from the beginning of World War II in Europe through the present, emphasizing significant movements, genres, and filmmakers.

title: Media Financing and Distribution
description: The processes of raising and budgeting funds and distributing film and videos for theatrical release, direct DVD/video release, or television broadcast.

title: Media Organization and Management
description: Principles and practices of the organization and management of electronic media and motion pictures.

title: Topics in Film Studies
description: Study of selected topics in fictive or nonfictive films, including genres, works of an individual director, or other emphasis. Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic changes.

title: Media Script Analysis
description: Analysis of the key structural and thematic elements of narrative screenplays.

title: Writing the Feature Film I
description: Advanced study of screenwriting with emphasis on the creation of a step outline for a feature length screenplay.

title: Writing the Feature Film II
description: A writing workshop in which students complete the first draft and a polish of a feature-length screenplay, based on the outline from MST 551 . Prerequisites: MST 551 .

title: Advanced Media Writing
description: Practice in television script writing with emphasis given to development of concepts and proposals for episodic television. Practice in analyzing and writing for existing television series and/or development of new programs.

title: Directing for Television
description: Fundamental principles of directing for television. Laboratory directing experience.

title: Dance on Video
description: Introduction to working with dance and video, including composing for the camera, recording dancers in action, and editing footage to create original work.

title: Animation Production
description: Study and practice of techniques of animation.

title: Documentary Production
description: Documentary construction, research, planning, and production techniques. Further development of video production skills in supervised laboratory project.

title: Research Extension   1-3
description: Research Extension.

title: Choral Music Teaching Lab
description: Meetings for all Choral/General Music Education majors. Presentations by music education majors, faculty and guests. See Student Information Manual. Notes: Grade: Pass/Not Pass (P/NP).

title: Introduction to Teaching Techniques in Choral/General Music
description: Students will explore sequential processes of musical development (skills, content, methods, techniques and materials). Includes onsite teaching practicum.

title: Introduction to Teaching Instrumental Music
description: Includes the fundamentals of teaching band, orchestra, and guitar in US public schools. Includes basic playing skills and instrument pedagogy for group instruction. Prerequisites: MEDU major with Choral/General Music Education concentration, and MUE 111 . or permission of instructor.

title: Introduction to Teaching Choral Music
description: Includes the fundamentals of teaching choir in US public schools. Includes basic singing skills and choral/vocal pedagogy for group instruction. Prerequisites: MEDU major with Instrumental/General Music Education concentration, Instrumental/General Jazz concentration, or Pop Tech Music Ed concentration. admission to the Teacher Education Program; junior standing or permission of instructor.

title: Foundations of Teaching for Musical Understanding
description: Overview of teaching musical concepts within a developmentally appropriate P-5 U. S. general music curricular framework. Emphasizes theories of learning and development in relation to music. Prerequisites: Either MUE 111 or MUE 363 or MUE 364 or MUE 365 or MUE 366 . or permission of instructor.

title: Jazz for Music Teachers
description: Introduction to jazz for future music teachers. Primary topics include historical study, listening, improvising, coaching ensembles, observation of jazz bands, and theoretical study. Prerequisites: Music Education majors only.

title: Foundations of Teaching Vocal Music
description: Teaching and organizing vocal music in the US public schools, with a focus on secondary school choral music materials, instruction, rehearsal strategy, and evaluation. Supervised teaching practicum. Content in individual musicianship, culturally-responsive teaching and exceptional learners will be explored in detail. Prerequisites: MUE 261 . admission to the Teacher Education Program; junior standing; or permission of instructor.

title: Marching Band Techniques
description: Study of US show-, corps-, and hybrid-style marching bands with emphasis on drill design, auxiliary units, and repertoire selection. Includes organization, personnel, equipment management, budgeting, and traveling with high school students. Prerequisites: Admission to the Teacher Education Program. junior standing or permission of instructor.

title: Music for Preschool and Elementary Teachers
description: Preschool and elementary school music and its place in U.S. P-5 curricula. Emphasis on music concepts, materials, teaching techniques, evaluation, and interrelationship of other areas of human creativity. Prerequisites: Junior standing or permission of instructor.

title: Teaching Strings Laboratory
description: Class instruction in playing and teaching violin, viola, cello, and bass. Includes basic playing skills on string instruments and preparation in string pedagogy for group instruction. Prerequisites: MEDU major or permission of instructor.

title: Teaching Brass Laboratory
description: Class instruction in playing and teaching brass instruments. Includes basic playing skills on brass instruments and preparation in brass pedagogy for group instruction. Prerequisites: MEDU major or permission of instructor.

title: Teaching Woodwinds Laboratory
description: Class instruction in playing and teaching woodwind instruments. Includes basic playing skills on woodwind instruments and preparation in woodwind pedagogy for group instruction. Prerequisites: MEDU major or permission of instructor.

title: Teaching Percussion Laboratory
description: Class instruction in playing and teaching percussion instruments. Includes basic playing skills on percussion instruments and preparation in percussion pedagogy for group instruction. Prerequisites: MEDU major or permission of instructor.

title: Foundations of Teaching Instrumental Music
description: Teaching and organizing instrumental music in the US public schools. Includes planning and implementing pedagogical techniques for the instrumental music classroom. Includes supervised school internship. Cultural diversity and exceptional learner content explored in detail. Prerequisites: MUE 261 , MUE 363 , MUE 364 , MUE 365 , and MUE 366 . admission to the Teacher Education Program; junior standing or permission of instructor.

title: Instrument Repair
description: Mechanics of brass, woodwind, percussion, and stringed instruments, plus laboratory time to develop skills in repairing instruments. Prerequisites: MUE 363 , MUE 364 , MUE 365 , and MUE 366 . or permission of instructor.

title: Choral Conducting II
description: Supervised practice in conducting choral rehearsals; comparative study of rehearsal procedures and of choral objectives; continued study of choral repertory. Prerequisites: MUP 319 or permission of instructor .

title: General Music in Elementary Schools
description: Instructional methods and approaches to promote P-5 learners' musical understanding. Includes supervised teaching practicum. Content in reading education, cultural diversity, and exceptional learners will be explored in detail. Prerequisites: MUE 261 . admission to the Teacher Education Program; junior or senior standing or permission of instructor.

title: Singing Voice in Classrooms and Ensembles
description: Provides an overview of vocal anatomy/function, care of the voice, vocal repertoire, teaching strategies, and other specialized topics related to singing instruction in music classrooms and choral settings. Prerequisites: MUE 261 .

title: Advanced String Methods
description: Overview of classroom methods teaching for teaching strings with an emphasis on advanced skills on secondary string instruments. Prerequisites: MUE 363 . sophomore standing or permission of instructor .

title: Choral Music in the Schools
description: Advanced treatment of choral curriculum, literature selection, and teaching/assessment of ensemble musicianship. Includes supervised teaching practicum. Content in reading education, cultural diversity, and exceptional learners will be explored in detail. Prerequisites: MUE 261 . admission to the Teacher Education Program; senior standing or permission of instructor.

title: Student Teaching in Music
description: Intensive, field-based internship in school music education. Includes practical applications of previous course work in supervised settings. Taught as Speaking Intensive Course. Prerequisites: MUE 461 or MUE 464 or MUE 467 . senior standing; passed advanced piano proficiency (MEDU major, choral/general concentration only); admission to the professional semester for student teaching; Corequisites: MUE 466 .

title: Student Teaching Seminar
description: Forum providing special education opportunities in conjunction with student teaching experiences. Emphasis placed on professional aspects of teaching. Reading education content explored in detail. Required: student teachers. Prerequisites: MUE 461 or MUE 464 or MUE 467 . senior standing; passed advanced piano proficiency (MEDU major, choral/general concentration only), admission to the professional semester for student teaching; Corequisites: MUE 465 .

title: Instrumental Music in Schools
description: Methods of teaching instrumental music; study of administrative procedures, repertoire, personnel, and resources; application of music teaching techniques. Reading education, cultural diversity, and exceptional learner content will be explored in detail. Prerequisites: MUE 261 , MUE 363 , MUE 364 , MUE 365 , MUE 366 , and MUE 367 . admission to the Teacher Education Program; senior standing or permission of instructor.

title: Instrumental Conducting II
description: Advanced instrumental conducting techniques, with emphasis on developing score study skills and movement fundamentals. Prerequisites: Successful completion of Conducting I ( MUP 372 or MUP 319 )  or permission of instructor.

title: Directed Study in Music  1-3
description: Supervised research in advanced subject area resulting in written document or composition. Project outline (available in Music office) prepared by student and supervising professor and approved by Associate Dean and Dean of School of Music. Prerequisites: Permission of supervising professor and Dean of School of Music. Notes: May be repeated for credit if topic varies.

title: Research Methods in Music
description: Writing skills, materials, and procedures utilized in music research; measurement, experimental design, theories/procedures for evaluation, statistics, computer applications, and initiation of scholarly research.

title: The Measurement of Musical Behavior
description: Review of standardized music tests and an examination of methods for developing tests related to the measurement of musical behavior.

title: Licensure Internship in Music Education
description: Overview of curriculum development, lesson planning, and assessment along with preparation towards completing EdTPA. Corequisites: MUE 657 or permission of instructor.

title: Student Teaching for Licensure
description: Intensive, field-based internship in a school music setting. Prerequisites: MUE 607 , MUE 657 . Corequisites: MUE 609 .

title: Licensure Seminar in Music Education
description: Seminar designed to mentor in-service alternate pathways teachers during student teaching. Emphasis placed on professional aspects of teaching. Prerequisites: MUE 607 , MUE 657 . Corequisites: MUE 608 .

title: Psychology of Music
description: The physical and psychological aspects of music involving human behavior.

title: Issues in Multicultural Music Education
description: Overview of historical, philosophical, cultural, and pedagogical issues in multicultural music education. Emphasis on the development of knowledge that will inform and enhance instructional practice.

title: Methods and Materials of Music Reading Instruction
description: Overview of methods and materials for music reading instruction. Emphasis on connections between language and music reading. Involves weekly online sessions, individual projects, and two related workshops.

title: Music for Exceptional People
description: Review of the learning styles of exceptional people and the implications for providing realistic musical activities throughout the lifespan. Emphasis will be placed on authentic and successful music participation. Notes: Non-music majors may enroll.

title: Contemporary Trends in Music Education
description: Current concepts and practices influencing contemporary music education, methods, and materials at the P-Collegiate level. Development of musical and pedagogical skills to include informal music learning and popular music.

title: Pedagogical Conducting
description: Advanced classroom conducting skills including baton technique, score reading, rehearsal techniques, score analysis, repertoire, programming, and interpretation. Prerequisites: Graduate standing in any Music degree program.

title: Teaching Elementary Music: Theory and Practice
description: Emphasis on strategies and materials for developing children's musicianship and problem-solving skills. Consideration of current child development and learning theories to teaching elementary general music. Prerequisites: Music teaching experience in elementary school or the equivalent of MUE 465 Student Teaching in Music.

title: Seminar in Music Education
description: Salient philosophies, practices, and methods involving music education as considered in a social context; challenges in dealing with elementary and secondary students and opportunities for music education in the community. Individual research project required.

title: Music Supervision
description: Philosophical foundations of music education as related to the development of supervisory and administrative programs. Basic concepts of music education and means for evaluation of music education curricula.

title: Advanced Choral and General Music Methods for the Adolescent
description: Examination of contemporary research and pedagogy with an emphasis on developing appropriate instructional strategies for use with adolescent students in choral and general music classrooms.

title: Advanced Music Teaching Techniques
description: Overview of current practice in learning and teaching music with emphasis on the development of practical strategies for implementation within music classrooms and studios of all types.

title: Philosophy of Music
description: Overview of historical and current philosophies of music. Specific applications will be made to various areas of expertise, including music composition, education, history, performance, and theory. Prerequisites: Admission to master's or doctoral program in music.

title: Portfolio Development in Music Education
description: Development of a portfolio that reflects the application of course work in music education to classroom teaching. Prerequisites: Acceptance in MM in Music Education or permission of instructor.

title: Portfolio Development in Music Education
description: Development of a portfolio that reflects the application of course work in music education to classroom teaching. Prerequisites: Acceptance in MM in Music Education or permission of instructor.

title: Thesis  1-6
description: Individual guidance in the development of a specific research problem.

title: Research Methods in Music
description: Writing skills, materials, and procedures utilized in music research; measurement, experimental design, theories/procedures for evaluation, statistics, computer applications, and initiation of scholarly research.

title: Research Methods in Music II
description: Advanced research methodology and procedures in music education. Prerequisites: MUE 700 .

title: Music and the Brain
description: An exploration of how music is processed in the brain from infancy through older age.

title: Creative Thinking in Music
description: Introduction to the major literature in creative thinking in music as it relates to music teaching and learning.  Projects include creative work in music, journal entries, short papers, teaching strategies.

title: College and University Music Administration
description: Provide basic information and skills for those who are in administrative assignments, who desire careers as music administrators, or who desire and understanding of the operations of a music unit.

title: Choral Practice Around the World
description: An introduction to the issues, materials and pedagogy that inform teaching vocal music from diverse cultures to ensembles or classrooms of trained and untrained singers.

title: Qualitative Research in Music Education
description: Various inquiry approaches as practiced specifically in music education including case study, narrative, phenomenology, grounded theory, and ethnography; completion of small-scale research study; development of writing and presentation skills.

title: The Measurement of Musical Behavior
description: Review of standardized music tests and an examination of methods for developing tests related to the measurement of musical behavior.

title: Psychology of Music
description: The physical and psychological aspects of music involving human behavior.

title: Issues in Multicultural Music Education
description: Overview of historical, philosophical, cultural, and pedagogical issues in multicultural music education. Emphasis on the development of knowledge that will inform and enhance instructional practice.

title: Methods and Materials of Music Reading Instruction
description: Overview of methods and materials for music reading instruction. Emphasis on connections between language and music reading. Involves weekly online sessions, individual projects, and two related workshops.

title: Music for Exceptional People
description: Review of the learning styles of exceptional people and the implications for providing realistic musical activities throughout the lifespan. Emphasis will be placed on authentic and successful music participation. Notes: Non-music majors may enroll.

title: Contemporary Trends in Music Education
description: Current concepts and practices influencing contemporary music education, methods, and materials at the P-Collegiate level. Development of musical and pedagogical skills to include informal music learning and popular music.

title: Pedagogical Conducting
description: Advanced classroom conducting skills including baton technique, score reading, rehearsal techniques, score analysis, repertoire, programming, and interpretation. Prerequisites: Graduate standing in any Music degree program.

title: Music Education in Society
description: An exploration of music education with particular attention to societal influences on teaching and learning music in the classroom and/or the studio.

title: Teaching Elementary Music: Theory and Practice
description: Emphasis on strategies and materials for developing children's musicianship and problem-solving skills. Consideration of current child development and learning theories to teaching elementary general music. Prerequisites: Music teaching experience in elementary school or the equivalent of MUE 465 Student Teaching in Music.

title: Seminar in Music Education
description: Salient philosophies, practices, and methods involving music education as considered in a social context; challenges in dealing with elementary and secondary students and opportunities for music education in the community. Individual research project required.

title: Music Supervision
description: Philosophical foundations of music education as related to the development of supervisory and administrative programs. Basic concepts of music education and means for evaluation of music education curricula.

title: Advanced Choral and General Music Methods for the Adolescent
description: Examination of contemporary research and pedagogy with an emphasis on developing appropriate instructional strategies for use with adolescent students in choral and general music classrooms.

title: Advanced Music Teaching Techniques
description: Overview of current practice in learning and teaching music with emphasis on the development of practical strategies for implementation within music classrooms and studios of all types.

title: Philosophy of Music
description: Overview of historical and current philosophies of music. Specific applications will be made to various areas of expertise, including music composition, education, history, performance, and theory. Prerequisites: Admission to master's or doctoral program in music.

title: Directed Study in Music  1-12
description: Supervised advanced research requiring a written document or composition.

title: Dissertation Extension  1-3
description: Dissertation Extension.

title: Experimental Course  1-6
description: This number reserved for experimental courses. Refer to the Course Schedule for current offerings.

title: Latin Jazz Ensemble
description: UNCG Latin Jazz Ensemble rehearses and performs Afro Cuban Jazz, Afro Rican Jazz, and other Afro Carribbean and South American Music such as Merengue, Bomba, Plena, and Bossa Nova. The ensemble develops, strengthens, and expands the performance and passion of Latin Jazz. Prerequisites: By audition only, or permission from instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: PopTech Ensemble
description: Pop Tech Ensembles develop each student’s musicianship through the rehearsal and performance of original compositions and arrangements of existing tunes. Open to all students who can play an instrument or sing. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor only. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Flute Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Clarinet Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Saxophone Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Violin Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Viola Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Camerata
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Prerequisites: PRFM, MEDU, or MUSI major. or permission of instructor; Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Casella Sinfonietta
description: Performance of conducted chamber literature for winds, strings, and percussion from all areas, including contemporary works for this medium. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. Corequisites: ENS 393 or ENS 391 . Notes: May be repeated every semester provided different repertoire is selected.

title: Chamber Music
description: Group study and performance of selected chamber music literature/jazz combo literature (nonconducted) with emphasis on development of independent chamber music performance skills. Personnel and repertoire assigned by performance faculty. Prerequisites: By audition or permission of instructor. Notes: May be repeated every semester provided different repertoire is selected.

title: Collaborative Piano
description: Students are assigned vocal and/or instrumental students to accompany with literature appropriate to the student's level. Fulfills large ensemble requirement for keyboard performance majors. Prerequisites: MUP 284 / MUP 285 or permission of instructor. Notes: May be repeated for a total of 5 semester hours.

title: Service Playing Skills for Organists and Pianists
description: Provides a setting in which music students will learn essential keyboard and professional skills for music positions at religious institutions as well as an understanding of how Western liturgical and non-liturgical traditions fit within global ecumenism and interfaith contexts. Prerequisites: Music major or minor. Organ or piano playing ability at the 300 level or above, or written permission of instructor; Corequisites: Current enrollment in studio organ or piano. May be waived by permission of the instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Historical Performance Consort
description: This course provides a setting in which music students will study and perform using principles, performance practices, and repertoire related to the historically informed performance of Western music of earlier ages, centering on the Baroque and Classical eras. Prerequisites: MUP 252 . Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Trumpet Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Prerequisites: MEDU or PRFM major. or permission of instructor; Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Horn Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Trombone Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Tuba/Euphonium Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Percussion Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Steelpan Ensemble  1-2
description: Performance of Steelpan music. Notes: May be repeated for credit as topic varies. New material/repertoire will be assigned each semester.

title: Latin American Folk Music Ensemble
description: The Latin American Folk and Popular Music Ensemble will explore the folk and popular song and dance styles of the Spanish speaking areas of the region, with a heavy focus on South American and Mexican music. Students will also learn about history and social significance of those styles. Prerequisites: None. Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic varies.

title: Present-Continuous New Music Ensemble  0-1
description: Provides advanced performers with the opportunity of performing contemporary repertoire as well as music of visiting composers and members of the UNCG community. The instrumentation of the ensemble varies from 4 to 18 performers. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit. Grade: Pass/Not Pass (P/NP).

title: Period Music Ensembles  1-2
description: Performance of conducted and nonconducted period (Renaissance through Contemporary) literature. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. Notes: Open to all university students. May be repeated provided different repertoire is selected.

title: Old Time Ensemble  1-2
description: Performance of traditional Old Time repertoire. Notes: Open to all university students. May be repeated provided different repertoire is selected.

title: Indian Music
description: Performance of Classical Indian Music. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Middle Eastern Ensemble
description: Performance of Arab, Turkish and other middle eastern musics. Notes: May be repeated for credit as topic varies. New material/repertoire will be assigned each semester.

title: Spartan Voices
description: Choral organization open to all undergraduate voices. Prerequisites: Open to all university students. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Spartan Voices II
description: Choral organization open to all voices. Prerequisites: Open to all university students. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: University Chorale
description: Mixed choral organization of approximately 50 graduate and undergraduate singers. Prerequisites: Membership by audition. Notes: Open to all university students. May be repeated for credit.

title: Touring Opera
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Prerequisites: PRFM, MEDU, or MUSI major. or permission of instructor; Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Opera Role  1-2
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Prerequisites: PRFM, MEDU, or MUSI major. or permission of instructor; Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Opera Chorus
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Prerequisites: PRFM, MEDU, or MUSI major. or permission of instructor; Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Schola Cantorum
description: Choral organization for graduate and undergraduate mixed voices. Prerequisites: Membership by audition. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Coro di Belle Voci
description: Choral Organization for graduate and undergraduate advanced soprano and alto voices. Prerequisites: Membership by audition. Notes: The course is open to all UNCG Sopranos and Altos. May be repeated every semester of enrollment for a total of 8 credit hours.

title: Chamber Singers
description: Select mixed vocal ensemble of 16 graduate and advanced undergraduate singers. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Sinfonia
description: String orchestra, performance of important works from symphonic repertoire of eighteenth, nineteenth, and twentieth centuries.

title: University Symphony Orchestra
description: Full symphony orchestra, performance of important works from symphonic repertoire of eighteenth, nineteenth, and twentieth centuries. Notes: Open to all university students. May be repeated for credit.

title: Concert Band
description: Large concert band for wind and percussion students performing standard repertoire and contemporary music for band. Open to all students without audition. Completion of course involves, in addition to the regular schedule of rehearsals, participation in public performances by the ensemble. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Wind Ensemble
description: Concert band for wind and percussion students specializing in the most advanced works for band including standard repertoire, contemporary music, and chamber literature. Open to all students by audition. Completion of course involves, in addition to the regular schedule of rehearsals, participation in public performances by the ensemble. Prerequisites: Membership by audition. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Symphonic Band
description: Large concert band for wind and percussion students performing advanced literature for band including standard repertoire and contemporary music. Open to all students by audition. Completion of course involves, in addition to the regular schedule of rehearsals, participation in public performances by the ensemble. Prerequisites: Membership by audition. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Jazz Ensemble I
description: Performance of literature encompassing all of the jazz idioms with emphasis on contemporary composition. Prerequisites: Membership by audition. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Jazz Ensemble II
description: Performance of literature encompassing all of the jazz idioms with emphasis on contemporary composition. Prerequisites: Membership by audition. Notes: May be repeated for credit.

title: Athletic Bands
description: The UNCG athletic bands perform music in a variety of styles at on-campus and off-campus events while inspiring audiences to support UNCG athletics and providing in-game leadership. Notes: May be repeated every semester.

title: Latin Jazz Ensemble
description: UNCG Latin Jazz Ensemble rehearses and performs Afro Cuban Jazz, Afro Rican Jazz, and other Afro Caribbean and South American Music such as Merengue, Bomba, Plena, and Bossa Nova. The ensemble develops, strengthens, and expands the performance and passion of Latin Jazz. Notes: By audition only.

title: Flute Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

title: Clarinet Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

title: Saxophone Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

title: Violin Ensemble

title: Camerata
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

title: Casella Sinfonietta
description: Chamber literature for winds, strings, and percussion from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. Corequisites: MUP 691 or MUP 693. Notes: May be repeated when repertoire varies.

title: Chamber Music  0-1
description: Group study and performance of selected chamber music literature/jazz combo literature (non-conducted) with emphasis on development of independent chamber music performance skills. Personnel and repertoire assigned by performance faculty. Prerequisites: Permission of Instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit when repertoire varies.

title: Service Playing Skills for Organists and Pianists
description: Provides a setting in which music students will learn essential keyboard and professional skills for music positions at religions institutions as well as an understanding of how Western liturgical and non-liturgical traditions fit within global ecumenism and interfaith contexts. Prerequisites: Permission of the instructor. Notes: Organ or piano playing ability equivalent to or greater than the undergraduate 300-level. Current enrollment in studio organ or piano required but may be waived by permission of the instructor.

title: Historical Performance Consort
description: Provides a setting in which music students will study principles, practices, instruments, repertoire, and current professional activities related to the historically informed performance of Western music of earlier ages, centering on the Baroque and Classical eras.

title: Trumpet Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

title: Trombone Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

title: Tuba/Euphonium Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

title: Percussion Ensemble
description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

title: Steelpan Ensemble
description: Performance of Steelpan Music. Notes: May be repeated for credit. New material/repertoire will be assigned each semester.

title: Latin American Folk Music Ensemble
description: Explores the folk and popular song and dance styles of the Spanish speaking areas of the region, with a heavy focus on South American and Mexican music. Students will also learn about history and social significance of those styles.

title: Present-Continuous New Music Ensmble  0-1
description: Provides advanced performers with the opportunity of performing contemporary repertoire as well as music of visiting composers and members of the UNCG community. The instrumentation of the ensemble varies from 4 to 18 performers. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor.

title: Period Music Ensembles
description: Performance of conducted and non-conducted period literature for winds, string, percussion, and voice. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit when different repertoire is selected.

title: Old Time Ensemble
description: Research, rehearsal, and performance of repertoire associated with Old Time Music. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit when different repertoire is selected.

title: Indian Music
description: Performance of classical Indian music.

title: Middle Eastern Ensemble
description: Performance of Arab, Turkish and other middle eastern musics. Notes: May be repeated for credit. New material/repertoire will be assigned each semester.

title: Spartan Voices
description: Choral organization open to all graduate voices.

title: Spartan Voices II
description: Choral organization open to all voices. Notes: Open to all university students. May be repeated for credit.

title: University Chorale
description: Mixed choral organization of approximately 50 singers. Notes: Membership by audition.

description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium.

description: Performance of literature from all eras, including contemporary works for this medium. Prerequisites: By audition.

description: Choral organization for graduate and undergraduate advanced soprano and alto voices. Audition is required or permission of instructor for admittance into the course.

title: Chamber Singers
description: Select mixed vocal ensemble of graduate and advanced undergraduate singers. Notes: Membership by audition.

title: Sinfonia
description: String orchestra, performance of important works from symphonic repertoire of eighteenth, nineteenth, and twentieth centuries.
