description: Introduces foundational pharmacology principles in nursing, emphasizing patient-centered care, clinical judgment, and medication safety. Students will explore drug mechanisms of action, therapeutic uses, and safe administration practices, preparing them to apply pharmacological knowledge effectively in clinical settings. Prerequisites: Admission to the prelicensure M.S.N. program. Corequisites: NUR 512 , NUR 513 , NUR 514 .

title: Psychiatric and Mental Health Nursing
description: Nursing care for mental health challenges across the lifespan with an emphasis on wellness, recovery, health promotion, patient advocacy, and pharmacological and non-pharmacological treatment modalities. Students will gain skills in mental health assessment, clinical judgment, and therapeutic communication. Prerequisites: NUR 512 , NUR 513 , NUR 514 , NUR 515 .

title: Person-Centered Care of Adults I
description: Introduction to adult health concepts, emphasizing person-centered, evidence-based care. Through care planning, clinical judgment, and collaboration, students will develop foundational skills for managing common adult health issues across various healthcare settings. Prerequisites: NUR 512 , NUR 513 , NUR 514 , NUR 515 .

title: Financial Management in Healthcare
description: Introduction to the principles of fiscal management, health care agency accounting practices, and the nurse administrator's role in budgeting processes. Prerequisites: Graduate students enrolled in MSN in Nursing Administration or MSN/MBA program.

title: Global Health and Disaster Preparedness Graduate Level
description: This course explores concepts in global health and disaster preparedness using a nation as a microcosm to explore these concepts. Prerequisites: Graduate student in good academic standing and instructor permission.

title: Critique and Utilization of Research in Nursing Administration
description: Development of knowledge and skill competencies basic to the critical interpretation and utilization of research findings in nursing administration. Graded on S/U basis. Prerequisites: NUR 641 .

title: Interprofessional/Interdisciplinary Collaboration and Negotiation
description: An integrated approach to team development and function using an interprofessional/interdisciplinary model.

title: The Role of the Nurse Leader in Advanced Quality and Safety
description: Prepares the student to apply principles of quality and safety at the micro (unit) and macro (organizational) system level. Prerequisites: Enrolled in the M.S.N. Nursing Leadership and Management Concentration, M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration, M.S.N./M.B.A., or permission of instructor.

title: Health and Illness in Infants, Children, and Adolescents
description: Nursing care and health promotion of infants, children, and adolescents with acute and chronic illness, special health care needs, and those receiving well child care. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration. Corequisites: NUR 611L .

title: Practicum for Health and Illness in Infants, Children, and Adolescents
description: Practicum for nursing care and health promotion of infants, children, and adolescents with acute and chronic illness, special health care needs, and those receiving well child care. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration. Corequisites: NUR 611 .

title: Childbearing and Reproductive Health
description: Nursing care of women and families along the childbearing continuum. Topics include reproductive health, promotion of health for pregnancy and birth, nursing management of complications, and newborn care. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration. Corequisites: NUR 613L .

title: Practicum for Childbearing and Reproductive Health
description: Practicum for nursing care of women and families along the childbearing continuum. Topics include reproductive health, promotion of health for pregnancy and birth, nursing management of complications, and newborn care. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration. Corequisites: NUR 613 .

title: Information Technology in Nursing Administration
description: Prepares the student to apply principles and techniques of communication and computer technology in health care management settings. Prerequisites: Enrolled in the M.S.N. Nursing Leadership and Management Concentration, M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration, M.S.N./M.B.A., or permission of instructor.

title: Nursing Leadership and Management
description: Provides the student with a foundational knowledge of management and leadership skills using applications based on problem-solving, decision-making, and critical thinking. Core management concepts include planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling. Other topics include legal and ethical decision making and advocacy. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration, NUR 501 , NUR 503 , NUR 503L , NUR 504 , NUR 504L , NUR 508 , NUR 509 , NUR 613 , NUR 613L , NUR 614 (or their equivalent).

title: Population Health Nursing
description: Nursing care of populations and communities through collaborative actions across care settings. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration, NUR 503 , NUR 503L , NUR 504 , NUR 504L , NUR 611 , NUR 611L , NUR 613 , NUR 613L (or their equivalent). Corequisites: NUR 618L .

title: Practicum for Population Health Nursing
description: Practicum for nursing care of populations and communities through collaborative actions across care settings. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration, NUR 503 , NUR 503L , NUR 504 , NUR 504L , NUR 611 , NUR 611L , NUR 613 , NUR 613L (or their equivalent). Corequisites: NUR 618 .

title: Nursing Care of Individuals with Acute Physiological Problems
description: Nursing care of individuals/families with acute physiological illness. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration, NUR 510 , NUR 510L , NUR 618 , NUR 618L , NUR 705 . Corequisites: NUR 617 , NUR 619L .

title: Practicum for Nursing Care of Individuals with Acute Physiological Problems
description: Practicum for nursing care of individuals/families with acute physiological illness. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration, NUR 510 , NUR 510L , NUR 618 , NUR 618L , NUR 705 . Corequisites: NUR 617 , NUR 619 .

title: The Business of Healthcare
description: A contemporary analysis of the U.S. health system from government, regulation, policy, insurance, and the economy is completed. Business approaches advocating for social and environmental justice are debated.

title: Senior Capstone
description: A concentrated 7 1/2-week practicum where students simulate the roles of the employed graduate under the guidance of a selected nursing preceptor and a faculty facilitator. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration, NUR 503 , NUR 503L , NUR 504 , NUR 504L , NUR 510 , NUR 510L , NUR 611 , NUR 611L , NUR 613 , NUR 613L , NUR 618 , NUR 618L , NUR 705 . Corequisites: NUR 622 .

title: Synthesis of Nursing Concepts
description: Cumulative integration of concepts learned throughout the curriculum. Emphasis placed on analysis of critical thinking skills and synthesis of clinical decision-making. Also includes preparation for NCLEX-RN. Prerequisites: Admission to M.S.N. Prelicensure Entry-Level Concentration, NUR 510 , NUR 510L , NUR 618 , NUR 618L , NUR 705 . Corequisites: NUR 609 , NUR 621 .

title: Leading in a Transforming Health System
description: Explores concept clarification associated with leading, managing, and following in the existing volatile, uncertain, complex and ambiguous U.S. health system. Foundational history from the Industrial Revolution to today’s emerging network models is synthesized against access, cost, and health disparities.

title: Project and Change Management
description: Leading change is a significant expectation for advancing the health system. Emphasis is placed on change and project management principles and the essential relationships between the change agent and client system. Further, strategies and techniques found in implementation science are practiced, reducing barriers to change and facilitating desired outcomes.

title: Foundations for Population Health
description: The influence of social, economic, cultural, and environmental factors on health outcomes and how they vary within groups are critiqued. The role of nurse administrators in evaluating care coordination, data organization, community engagement, and interventions to ensure equity are examined.

title: Quality Improvement in Principles and Practice
description: Prepares nurse leaders to understand and apply principles of quality improvement and their effects in health systems. Data-driven decision-making and technology integration into improvement models are tested.

title: Applied Budgeting and Resources Management
description: Prepares nurse leaders to utilize financial terminology, reimbursement models, and reporting methods. Special attention is paid to staffing, capital, operating budget development, and variance analysis in health care organizations.

title: Leadership and Management Essentials in Healthcare: Theories and Roles
description: Nursing management within the health care system.  Management functions of planning, organizing and staffing studied within the framework of leadership in health care organizations. Prerequisites: NUR 715 . Corequisites: NUR 715 .

title: Organizational Leadership and Management
description: Management functions of directing and controlling are studied within the framework of leadership in health care organizations. Prerequisites: NUR 540 , NUR 614 , NUR 641 . Corequisites: NUR 614 .

title: Strategic Management of Health Care Organizations
description: Application of leadership and administration knowledge in a practice setting while defining self in a professional leadership role. Prerequisites: NUR 644 .

title: Nursing Leadership and Management Practicum
description: Application of advanced leadership and management knowledge in a practice setting while defining self in a professional leadership roles. Prerequisites: NUR 645 .

title: Designing Care Delivery Roles and Functions
description: Elements comprising a care delivery model are investigated, including patient-centered vs. patient-sensitive care, population vs. individual care needs, nurse-led vs. physician-led practices, and professional governance vs. centralized or authoritarian governance. Generational shifts to accommodate workforce shortages, increased patient demands, and associated human resource regulations are analyzed.

title: Science-Based Administrative Practice
description: Research-based course introducing learners to scientific processes used to study organizational and clinical practice, the interpretation of scientific findings to guide clinical management, policy setting, and decision-making, and the unique challenges of conducting research in the non-laboratory setting.  Emphasis on critique and application of research findings is presented.

title: Advancing Innovation Through Design Thinking
description: Designed for nurse leaders passionate about driving positive change in healthcare through innovative thinking and problem-solving. The focus is to empower leaders to address complex challenges, enhance patient care, and drive positive organizational change through a human-centered approach.

title: Environmental Health Impact on Population Well-being
description: Climate, disasters and the impact on health are critiqued. Pathophysiology of physical and mental well-being and material supply chains and management are analyzed. Epidemiology and resource management roles are addressed.

title: Practicum in Administrative Practice
description: The practicum allows learners to synthesize didactic content and experiential learning assignments and gain additional administrative experiences to validate program competencies. Students will work with their preceptor to complete a semester long leadership project.

title: Reproductive Health
description: Explores the care requirements associated with pregnancy, childbirth, postpartum, gender health, and neonatal care. Addresses diverse family structures, cultural variations, and ethical considerations in fertility, pregnancy, and genetics. Students will apply physiological knowledge and assess maternal and neonatal health while navigating moral-ethical considerations in reproductive care. Prerequisites: NUR 512 , NUR 513 , NUR 514 , NUR 515 .

title: Pediatric Care and Family Dynamics
description: Nursing care of the pediatric patient across infancy, childhood, and adolescence with a focus on health promotion, disease prevention, acute and chronic health conditions and associated risk factors, and the well child. Applying a family-centered approach with consideration of developmental, cultural, social, and psychological needs of the patient and family. Prerequisites: NUR 512 , NUR 513 , NUR 514 , NUR 515 .

title: Population and Community Health Principles
description: Explores population health principles through community engagement, with a focus on health promotion, disease prevention, health advocacy, and addressing social determinants of health. It covers the spectrum of healthcare delivery, from prevention to disease management, emphasizing collaboration with partners and stakeholders to improve health outcomes for diverse populations. Prerequisites: NUR 512 , NUR 513 , NUR 514 , NUR 515 .

title: Person-Centered Care of Adults II
description: Refines students' clinical judgment, decision-making, and problem-solving skills. Emphasizing individualized care, students will explore how to apply theory to practice for complex adult health issues. Through assessment, care planning, and collaboration, students will enhance their ability to deliver holistic, evidence-based interventions and improve patient outcomes in diverse environments. Prerequisites: NUR 517 .

title: Family Centered Care
description: Examines person-centered nursing care of families experiencing acute and chronic illnesses, disability, and loss as a result of complex health conditions. Emphasis is placed on integrating compassion, communication, and collaboration into family care while considering health disparities, social determinants, and transitions of care.

title: Psychopharmacology for Advanced Practice
description: Explores psychopharmacological treatment for psychiatric, mental, behavioral, and substance abuse disorders across the lifespan, covering pharmacodynamics, pharmacokinetics, major drug classes, indications, dosing, side effects, and interactions. Students learn evidence-based medication management strategies and recent advances in neurobiology and psychopharmacology for advanced nursing practice in diverse clinical settings. Prerequisites: Advanced pharmacology or permission of instructor. Corequisites: NUR 677 , NUR 678 .

title: Assessment and Diagnosis of Psychiatric and Mental Health Disorders Across the Lifespan
description: Develops skills in comprehensive assessment and diagnosis of psychiatric and mental health disorders across the lifespan. It emphasizes diagnostic reasoning, case formulation, treatment planning, and understanding neuroanatomy and brain-behavior principles to manage behavioral health and psychiatric issues. Preparing students to address normal and abnormal behaviors across diverse populations. Prerequisites: Advanced health assessment or permission of instructor. Corequisites: NUR 676 , NUR 678 .

title: Psychotherapy: Theory and Practice for Individuals and Groups
description: Integrates psychotherapy theories and practical application, preparing students to deliver effective care across the lifespan. Students explore cognitive-behavioral, psychodynamic, humanistic, and integrative approaches, focusing on assessment, therapeutic relationships, and treatment planning. Emphasis is on therapy stages and evidence-based interventions for individuals and groups, applying theoretical and therapeutic approaches. Corequisites: NUR 676 , NUR 677 .

title: Managing Complex Psychiatric Disorders in Adults and Families
description: Integrates psychotherapeutic and psychopharmacological interventions for managing complex psychiatric disorders in adults and families across the lifespan. Emphasis is on treating mood and behavioral symptoms, co-occurring disorders, and developing comprehensive, culturally competent care plans for multifaceted cases. Prerequisites: NUR 676 , NUR 677 , or permission of instructor. Corequisites: NUR 690 .

title: Psychiatric Mental Health Clinical
description: Advanced clinical course enhances competencies in delivering safe, ethical psychiatric care across the lifespan through supervised practice. Emphasis is on collaborative management of complex disorders and co-occurring conditions, developing evidence-based care plans. Students refine clinical judgment, diagnostic skills, therapeutic communication, and leadership while integrating assessment and pharmacological management. Prerequisites: NUR 676 , NUR 677 , or permission of instructor. Corequisites: NUR 679 .

title: Managing Complex Psychiatric Disorders in Special and Vulnerable Populations
description: Enhances students’ knowledge of diagnosing and treating psychiatric disorders in vulnerable populations, including children, adolescents, older adults, individuals with chronic illnesses, substance use and personality disorders, those in the criminal justice system, refugees, LGBT+ individuals, and military populations. Students will confront their biases and values in psychiatric practice. Prerequisites: NUR 676 or permission of instructor.

title: Independent Study   1-3
description: Guided readings, research, and individual project work under the direction of the School of Nursing faculty. Notes: May be used as part of minimum 36 hours required for the MSN degree. May be repeated for a maximum of 6 hours credit.

title: Integrative Psychiatric-Mental Health Clinical
description: Culminating experience for psychiatric mental health nurse practitioner students. Skill refinement and responsibility in delivering comprehensive psychiatric care across the lifespan is emphasized. Through precepted practicum experiences, students demonstrate proficiency by integrating knowledge, building confidence and preparing for independent, ethical, and collaborative practice as PMHNPs. Prerequisites: NUR 691 or permission of instructor.

title: Immersive Clinical Practicum
description: Synthesizes course and clinical experiences, focusing on time management, health promotion, acute and rehabilitative care, and reducing gaps in care transitions. Students emerge as novice nurse generalists, applying program knowledge to provide safe and effective care while optimizing community resources. Prerequisites: NUR 669 . Corequisites: NUR 695 .

title: Transition to Practice
description: A systematic review of topics associated with NCLEX are evaluated. Test-taking principles are reinforced, and sample tests provide areas for improved continuous learning. Personal and professional development opportunities are reviewed, allowing for program outcome evaluation and effective role transition from student to a practice-ready nurse. Corequisites: NUR 694 .

title: Advanced Nursing Project  1-6
description: Scholarly inquiry project developed by the student with faculty guidance. Prerequisites: NUR 705 . Notes: May be repeated for a maximum of 6 hours credit. Grading method is Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U).

title: Thesis   1-6
description: Individual guidance and direction in research to address a clinical or nonclinical problem. Prerequisites: NUR 705 . Notes: May be repeated for a maximum of 6 hours credit. Grading method is Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U).

title: Statistical Applications for Nursing Research
description: Applied descriptive and inferential statistics for nursing and health care research. Principles of hypothesis testing, inference, estimation, correlation, regression, and analysis of variance. Emphasis on selection of analysis and interpretation of results. Prerequisites: Admission to Ph.D. in Nursing and completion of a course in inferential statistics or permission of instructor.

title: Statistical Applications for Nursing Research II
description: Advanced statistics for nursing and health care research, including general linear model, multifactoral ANOVA, multiple regression. Emphasis on selection and conduct of analysis and interpretation of results. Prerequisites: NUR 701 or permission of instructor.

title: Qualitative Methods for Nursing
description: Philosophy, theory, and methods of qualitative research with an emphasis on the application to nursing and health research. Prerequisites: NUR 702 and NUR 712 or permission of instructor.

title: Quantitative Research in Nursing
description: Theoretical foundations, design, and techniques of quantitative research for nursing and health care. Prerequisites: NUR 702 and NUR 712 , or permission of instructor.

title: Scientific Approaches to Advance Healthcare
description: The critical inquiry to problem-solving is explored. Research methods guide questioning, retrieving, analyzing data, and generating science-based knowledge relevant to advanced nursing practice.

title: Measurement in Nursing and Healthcare Research
description: Measurement of biophysical, psychomotor, cognitive, affective,  and system-level phenomena for nursing and health research. Reliability, validity, item analysis, norms, selection and development of measures. Prerequisites: NUR 704 or permission of instructor.

title: Advanced Pathophysiology
description: Human physiologic changes and common pathophysiological and compensatory mechanisms are applied as a basis for advanced practice diagnostic reasoning and deliberation.

title: Pharmacologic Interventions in Advanced Practice
description: Drug therapies are applied to various health conditions, and their dosing, side effects, and drug-to-drug interactions are analyzed for efficacy and cost.

title: Philosophical and Theoretical Analysis in Nursing Science
description: The philosophical underpinnings and theoretical frameworks that guide nursing science. Analysis and evaluation of theory related to phenomena of interest.

title: Diagnostic Reasoning and Health Assessment
description: A foundational course to build on the skills of conducting comprehensive and systematic assessment of individuals across the lifespan. An introduction to diagnostic reasoning, laboratory studies, and other diagnostic tests are presented. Prerequisites: Baccalaureate-level health assessment course. Corequisites: NUR 713L .

title: Interventional Strategies of Diagnostic Reasoning and Health Assessment Lab
description: Application of advanced health assessment and diagnostic reasoning skills using laboratory exercises on individuals across the lifespan. Corequisites: NUR 713 .

title: Foundations of Informatics and Computational Systems
description: Biomedical computer technologies to support clinical problem solving and decision-making are applied. Emphasis on emerging technologies/artificial intelligence is analyzed for its impact on quality care and moral/ethical ramifications.

title: Research Methods in Nursing
description: Examines the basic concepts, strategies, and procedures used in conducting and analyzing nursing research. Emphasis will be on proposal development and quantitative and qualitative analysis of data. Prerequisites: An undergraduate statistics course (preferred within past 5 years).

title: Management of Child Health in Primary Care
description: Focuses on the assessment, diagnosis, and management of pediatric conditions and preventative care from birth through middle childhood encountered in primary care. Prerequisites: NUR 718 , NUR 812 , NUR 812L . Corequisites: NUR 719L .

title: Complex Problems In Family Primary Care
description: Students will increase knowledge and decision-making skills in the primary care treatment across the lifespan of vulnerable and complex patients. Course includes laboratory interpretation. Prerequisites: NUR 811 , NUR 811L . Corequisites: NUR 812 , NUR 812L .

title: Family Primary Care Practicum
description: Practicum focused on the application of scientific knowledge and clinical judgement in assessment, diagnosis, management of health conditions and wellness in primary care across the lifespan. Prerequisites: NUR 718 , NUR 812 , NUR 812L . Corequisites: NUR 716 .

title: Basic Principles of Nurse Anesthesia Practice I
description: General introduction to basics of nurse anesthesia practice, including safety, assessment, pharmacology, monitoring, charting, positioning, care plan development, sequencing, and as a foundation to more advanced anesthetic concepts and techniques.

title: Basic Principles of Nurse Anesthesia Practice Lab
description: Provides nurse anesthesia graduate students with an introduction to the principles of clinical anesthesia practice. Engages students in a variety of simulation and case study activities addressing the fundamentals of safe anesthesia practice across the spectrum of anesthesia care. Prerequisites: NUR 727 . Corequisites: NUR 734 .

title: Physics, Chemistry, and Mechanics in Anesthesia
description: Focuses on the application of the physical laws of gas, the study of compressed gases for clinical use, and the physical and mechanical considerations of anesthesia delivery systems.

title: Physics, Chemistry, and Mechanics in Anesthesia Laboratory
description: Focuses on the application of the physical laws of gas, the study of compressed gases for clinical use, and the physical and mechanical considerations of anesthesia delivery systems. Prerequisites: NUR 728 .

title: Health Assessment for Nurse Anesthesia
description: Emphasizes the principles of patient assessment for nurse anesthesia practice in the pre and perioperative periods. Corequisites: NUR 729L .

title: Health Assessment Laboratory for Nurse Anesthesia
description: Laboratory course emphasizing the principles of patient assessment for nurse anesthesia practice in the pre and perioperative periods. Prerequisites: NUR 729 .

title: Neuropathophysiology of Nurse Anesthesia
description: Focuses on the anatomy, physiology, and pathophysiology of the central nervous system, and anesthesia nursing care related to persons with neurological dysfunction.

title: Advanced Principles of Nurse Anesthesia Practice
description: Focuses on the principles and theories of advanced nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia for complex adult and pediatric patients. Prerequisites: NUR 727 , NUR 734 .

title: Advanced Principles of Nurse Anesthesia Practice Lab
description: Engages students in a variety of simulation and case study activities that address advanced anesthesia practice across the spectrum of anesthesia care. Prerequisites: NUR 727 , NUR 727L , NUR 734 . Corequisites: NUR 732 .

title: Basic Principles of Nurse Anesthesia Practice II
description: General introduction to the basics of nurse anesthesia practice, including safety, assessment, airway management, considerations for specialized populations, crises management, substance use disorder, care plan development, and as a foundation to more advanced anesthetic concepts and techniques.

title: Professional Aspects of Nurse Anesthesia
description: Examines nurse anesthesia practice and the CRNA role by analyzing the specialty-specific philosophical bases of caring, professionalism, advocacy, scope of practice, policy and standards of care in anesthesia practice, the role of professional organizations, legal and regulatory issues in anesthesia practice, billing, and malpractice.

title: Anesthesia Crisis Resource Management
description: Provides nurse anesthetist graduate students with principles of crisis resource management and evidence-based techniques within an advanced, high-fidelity simulation environment.

title: Pharmacology of Nurse Anesthesia I
description: Advanced knowledge of pharmacologic principles related to anesthetic medications.

title: Biochemistry for Nurse Anesthesia
description: Advanced knowledge of the biochemical basis and measurement of homeostasis and nursing interventions for fluid and electrolyte imbalance. Prerequisites: NUR 756 , NUR 757 , NUR 773A .

title: Cardiovascular Pathophysiology for Nurse Anesthesia
description: Focuses on the principles and theories of advanced nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia to individuals with cardiovascular dysfunction. Prerequisites: NUR 727 , NUR 732 . Corequisites: NUR 754 .

title: Pharmacology of Nurse Anesthesia II
description: Focuses on advanced nurse anesthesia knowledge of the pharmacology of cardiovascular drugs. Prerequisites: NUR 751 .

title: Respiratory Pathophysiology for Nurse Anesthesia
description: Focuses on the principles and theories of advanced nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia to individuals with respiratory dysfunction.

title: Pharmacology of Nurse Anesthesia III
description: Focuses on advanced nurse anesthesia knowledge of local anesthetics, neuromuscular blocking drugs, and adjunct medications necessary to effectively administer anesthesia to a diverse population.

title: Pathophysiology of Abdominal Systems for Nurse Anesthesia  2,3
description: Advanced knowledge of pathophysiology and anesthesia nursing care related to persons with hepatic, renal, and endocrine dysfunction.

title: Pharmacology of Nurse Anesthesia IV
description: Focuses on the principles of advanced nurse anesthesia knowledge of antimicrobials, adjunct agents, and concurrent outpatient drug therapy necessary to effectively administer anesthesia to a diverse population. Prerequisites: NUR 757 .

title: Clinical Practicum in Nurse Anesthesia I
description: Focuses on the delivery of basic nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia for a diverse patient population in a variety of institutional settings. Prerequisites: NUR 727 , NUR 728 , NUR 729 , NUR 730 .

title: Clinical Practicum in Nurse Anesthesia II  6,8
description: Focuses on the delivery of basic nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia for a diverse patient population in a variety of institutional settings. Prerequisites: NUR 771A .

title: Clinical Practicum in Nurse Anesthesia III  6,7
description: Focuses on the delivery of advanced nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia for a diverse patient population in a variety of institutional settings. Prerequisites: NUR 771A , NUR 771B . Corequisites: NUR 771A , NUR 771B .

title: Clinical Practicum in Nurse Anesthesia IV
description: Focuses on the delivery of complex nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia for a diverse patient population in a variety of institutional settings. Prerequisites: NUR 772 .

title: Clinical Practicum in Nurse Anesthesia V  6,7
description: Focuses on the delivery of advanced nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia for a diverse patient population in a variety of institutional settings. Prerequisites: NUR 772 , NUR 773A .

title: Clinical Practicum in Nurse Anesthesia VI  6,7
description: Focuses on the delivery of increasingly complex nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia for a diverse patient population in a variety of institutional settings. Prerequisites: NUR 773A , NUR 773B .

title: Clinical Practicum in Nurse Anesthesia VII  6,8
description: Focuses on the integration of basic, advanced, and complex nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia for a diverse patient population in a variety of institutional settings. Prerequisites: NUR 774 .

title: Clinical Practicum in Nurse Anesthesia VIII  6,7
description: Focuses on the integration of basic, advanced, and complex nurse anesthesia practice necessary to effectively administer anesthesia for a diverse patient population in a variety of institutional settings. Prerequisites: NUR 775A .

title: Special Topics in Nursing Science  1-3
description: In depth topics of special interest related to nursing and health sciences. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor.

title: Health Disparities and Outcomes
description: Critical analysis of health disparities and outcomes. Differences in access, use, cost, and quality of health care services; causes of health disparities; and strategies to eliminate disparities. Prerequisites: NUR 781 or permission of instructor. Corequisites: NUR 704 .

title: Advanced Health Policy and Ethics
description: Advanced analysis and evaluation of domestic and international regulation, financing, and delivery of health care; ethical issues and stakeholders that influence policy; evaluation of policies for selected populations. Prerequisites: NUR 782 or permission of instructor.

title: Doctor of Nursing Practice Advanced Practicum  1-6
description: Faculty supervised direct and indirect practice experiences that prepare D.N.P. students to function in advanced nursing roles. Includes practice experiences that integrate previous skills, promote innovative thinking, and test potential solutions to clinical practice or system issues. Emphasizes practice roles commensurate with health system needs and improved healthcare outcomes. Prerequisites: Previous practicum hours in an accredited M.S.N. program.

title: Directed Research
description: Directed research that culminates in a scholarly product. Prerequisites: NUR 704 and prior approval of graduate faculty member who will supervise and evaluate the project.

title: Literature Review and Synthesis in Research
description: Prepares the student to conduct a rigorous/systematic review of the literature, synthesize the literature, and draft a literature review manuscript in an area of interest. Prerequisites: Completion of the first semester of the Ph.D. program or permission of the instructor.

title: Independent Study   1-3
description: Prerequisite: Permission of instructor, Director of Graduate Study and Dean of Graduate School; Notes: Grade: Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U).

title: Grant Writing
description: Grant writing for nurse scientists. Prerequisites: Completion of the first year of the Ph.D. program or permission of the instructor.

title: Integrated Clinical Concepts
description: This course focuses on the integration of all aspects of nurse anesthesia practice and preparation for the national certification examination given by the National Board on Certification and Recertification. Prerequisites: NUR 732 , NUR 752 , NUR 753 , NUR 755, NUR 756 , NUR 758 , NUR 759 . Corequisites: NUR 775B .

title: Dissertation  1-12
description: Individual direction for the completion of doctoral dissertation research. Prerequisites: Satisfactory completion of written and oral comprehensive examinations and written permission of dissertation chair.

title: Dissertation Extension  1-3
description: Dissertation Extension. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor.

title: Research Extension  1-3
description: Research Extension. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor.

title: Advanced Research Methods
description: Exposure to and experience with complex research methods that address emerging issues in nursing and health sciences knowledge. Course content addresses multiple areas of emerging and innovative research. Prerequisites: NUR 702 , NUR 703 , NUR 704 , NUR 707 or equivalents, or permission of instructor.

title: Interventional Strategies to Promote Population Wellbeing
description: Mechanisms to improve health status and outcomes within populations, appraised and used to reverse negative social determinants of health. Examining population health, health equity, health promotion, disease prevention, and community-based interventions across the lifespan.

title: Advanced Primary Care Management I
description: Contrasting nursing and medical care models are introduced, addressing disease prevention and health promotion approaches. Acute and common chronic health issues, their etiology, and evidence-based treatment options are examined across the lifespan. Prerequisites: NUR 708 , NUR 713 , NUR 713L . Corequisites: NUR 709 , NUR 811L .

title: Advanced Primary Care Management I Practicum
description: The first in a series of clinical courses. Practicum for management of acute and chronic illness with emphasis on disease prevention and health promotion across the lifespan. Prerequisites: NUR 708 , NUR 709 , NUR 713 , NUR 713L . Corequisites: NUR 709 , NUR 811 .

title: Advanced Primary Care Management II
description: All life stages, from birth to older adult, and their corollary health and illness trajectories are analyzed. Advanced assessment and interventions are developed to include a team-based approach to care management across the lifespan. Prerequisites: NUR 709 , NUR 811 , NUR 811L . Corequisites: NUR 812L .

title: Advanced Primary Care Management II Practicum
description: Practicum for management of acute and chronic illness with emphasis on disease prevention and health promotion across the lifespan. Prerequisites: NUR 709 , NUR 811 , NUR 811L . Corequisites: NUR 812 .

title: Primary Care Management for Aging or Frail Adults
description: The complexities of acute to chronic disease, conditions of aging, and methods to aid older to frail adults and their families are assessed, managed, and evaluated to promote dignity and comfort. Prerequisites: NUR 709 , NUR 812 , NUR 812L . Corequisites: NUR 813L .

title: Primary Care Management for Aging or Frail Adults Practicum
description: Practicum for assessment, management, and evaluation of older adults and frail adults by the Adult Gerontological Nurse Practitioner in primary care. Prerequisites: NUR 812 , NUR 812L . Corequisites: NUR 813 .

title: Integrated Clinical Management for the Advanced Practice Nurse in Primary Care
description: Advanced primary care practicum emphasizing the ability to manage acute and chronic complex health conditions of adults demonstrating advanced critical decision making skills and integration of DNP essentials into advanced practice. Prerequisites: NUR 812 , NUR 813 . Corequisites: NUR 816L .

title: Clinical Synthesis Practicum for Advanced Practice Nurses  2-4
description: Leadership, care management, and clinical practice are synthesized and applied. This practicum is population-based, focusing on decision-making, and interprofessional care delivered to individuals with acute and chronic conditions emphasizing disease prevention and health promotion among adolescents to older adults. Prerequisites: NUR 811L , NUR 812L , NUR 813L . Corequisites: NUR 816 .

title: Negotiating Role Functions in an Advanced Practice Role
description: The alignment of advanced practice opportunities with career aspirations is mapped, with attention to negotiating, fulfilling, and optimizing knowledge, skills, and abilities in a selected position.

title: Biostatistical Basis for Decision-Making
description: Tools and techniques to interpret and measure treatment effectiveness, disease incidence and prevalence, and the efficacy of research findings are applied to population-level decision-making. Prerequisites: Admission to D.N.P. program and undergraduate statistics course.

title: Holistic Approaches to Leadership
description: Individual and group behavior, foundations for organizational development, and leading in new economy networks will cultivate leadership capacity to lead and manage complex change. Prerequisites: Admission to D.N.P. program.

title: Seminar for Nurse Anesthesia Advanced Practice
description: Focuses on the analysis of professional issues affecting anesthesia nursing practice.

title: Comprehensive Clinical Practicum
description: This clinical course is the synthesis of key clinical concepts that allows students to demonstrate knowledge and comprehension of the role of an advanced practice nurse. Prerequisites: NUR 816 , NUR 816L .

title: Applied Theory in Advancing Practice
description: Theory development and relevance to advance clinical and organizational practice is reinforced through curated exemplars. Seminal and emerging theories are discovered and analyzed for relevant practice interests.

title: Analysis and Application of DNP Essentials: Clinical Scholarship and Analytical Methods for Evidence
description: Analysis and integration of clinical scholarship and analytical methods for evidence-based practice applied to the role of the D.N.P. in nursing practice. Prerequisites: Admission to the post master's D.N.P. program.

title: Professional Advocacy and Networking in Healthcare
description: Structural and relational networks are studied to advocate for access to care and to promote a seamless and cost-effective healthcare system. Interprofessional opportunities for advocacy are promoted. Prerequisites: Admission to the graduate program.

title: Scholarly Seminar I: Project Development
description: A high-impact population-based problem will be articulated through organizational analysis, evidenced-based practice, standards identification, and metrics, to measure desired outcomes. Quality improvement techniques are reviewed to augment analysis and project implementation. Prerequisites: NUR 705 , NUR 818 , NUR 824 .

title: Scholarly Seminar II: Project Implementation
description: Implementation science and quality improvement principles are applied to a project plan, emphasizing the change agent-client system relationship, environmental drivers, and impediments to sustainable change. Prerequisites: NUR 890A .

title: Scholarly Seminar III: Evaluation, Conclusion, and Dissemination
description: Evaluate and disseminate project outcomes to intra- and interprofessional peers and populations of interest using print and digital materials, professional publications, and speaking strategies. Prerequisites: NUR 890B .

title: Applied Evidence-Based Practice & Translational Methods Project I
description: An applied evidence-based practice and translational methods project to be completed sequentially in a five-course series. Prerequisites: NUR 705 .

title: Applied Evidence-Based Practice  & Translational Methods Project II
description: An applied evidence-based practice and translational methods project to be completed sequentially in a five-course series. Prerequisites: NUR 898A .

title: Applied Evidence-Based Practice & Translational Methods Project III
description: An applied evidence-based practice and translational methods project to be completed sequentially in a five-course series. Prerequisites: NUR 898B .

title: Applied Evidence-Based Practice & Translational Methods Project IV
description: An applied evidence-based practice and translational methods project to be completed sequentially in a five-course series. Prerequisites: NUR 898C .

title: Applied Evidence-Based Practice & Translational Methods Project V
description: An applied evidence-based practice and translational methods project to be completed sequentially in a five-course series. Prerequisites: NUR 898D .

title: Find Your Way in Nutrition
description: This course will promote cohesiveness among first year Nutrition students and Nutrition faculty, relay information regarding the multiple paths available to and job opportunities for Nutrition graduates, and orient students to and help familiarize them with the department. Prerequisites: Must be enrolled in one of the following Fields of Study (Major): Nutrition. Notes: Grade: Pass/Not Pass (P/NP).

title: Introduction to Food Science
description: Basic scientific principles of food preparation with emphasis upon the science of food, standards of selection, purchasing, preparation, storage, preservation, and sustainability.

title: Culinary Skills for Food Selection and Menu Planning
description: Basic culinary skills for food selection and menu planning developed through activities that stress adapting standard recipes, measurement conversions, culinary calculations, standards of food selection, and cooking methods. Prerequisites: Grade of C- or better in MAT 115 or MAT 118 . NUTR major or minor.

title: Introductory Nutrition
description: Basic principles of human nutrition with emphasis on the nutrients and factors which affect their utilization in the human body. MAC: MAC Health and Wellness

title: Introduction to Dietetics
description: An overview of dietetics profession that will cover scope of practice, code of ethics, resources for evidence based practice, professional organizations, career options, professional development, and professional trends. Prerequisites: Nutrition major.

title: Nutrition Education and Application Processes
description: Study of communication of nutrition science through nutrition education, professional literature, and public media. Evaluation and use of professional and scientific literature in nutrition and food systems. Prerequisites: Grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 213 . Grade of C- or better in ENG 101 ; Written permission required.

title: Nourish Kitchen: Cooking with Nutrition in Mind
description: Students learn practical skills to apply nutrition concepts that may impact their own life and those around them through culinary medicine principles bridging the gap between nutrition science and healthy, delicious cooking. Prerequisites: NTR 213 with a grade of C or higher.

title: Quantity Food Procurement and Production
description: Procurement and production of quantity foods with an emphasis on menu planning, pre-preparation, service, sanitation, delivery systems, selection, use, and care of quantity-food equipment. Prerequisites: Grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 203 . Written permission required; Corequisites: NTR 309L . Notes: Professional liability insurance required.

title: Nutrition Throughout the Life Cycle
description: Principles of nutrition applied to meet the nutrient needs at different stages of the life cycle. Forces governing food availability, acceptability, nutritive quality and safety are stressed in the preparation of nutritional plans for individuals and groups. Prerequisites: Grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 213 or permission of instructor .

title: Micronutrients: Assessment, Bioavailability & Deficiencies
description: This course focuses on vitamins and minerals, including their food sources, dietary requirements, biological functions, and symptoms of deficiency and toxicity. Methods of assessment, including dietary, biochemical, and clinical, will also be addressed. Prerequisites: C- or higher in NTR 213 . C- or higher in BIO 111 ; C- or higher in BIO 111L ; C- or higher in CHE 103 or CHE 111 .

title: Supervised Professional Experience  1-4
description: Supervised professional experience in selected commercial or industrial organizations, public or private agencies in accordance with the major course of study of the student.

title: Special Problems in Nutrition  1-4
description: Individual study. Conference hours to be arranged.

title: Integrated Food Science and Culinary Medicine
description: Lecture covering experimental study of factors regulating the preparation of standard food products, their impact on human health, and review of current developments in food technology. Prerequisites: Grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 103 , NTR 203 , NTR 213 , NTR 302 . Grade of C- or better in CHE 110 and CHE 101 or CHE 103 or CHE 111 ; Written permission required; Corequisites: NTR 403L .

title: Integrated Food Science and Culinary Medicine Laboratory
description: Laboratory covering experimental study of factors regulating the preparation of standard food products, their impact on human health, and review of current developments in food technology. Prerequisites: Grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 103 , NTR 203 , NTR 213 , and NTR 302 . Grade of C- or better in CHE 110 and CHE 101 or CHE 103 or CHE 111 ; Corequisites: NTR 403 . Notes: All types of foods will be prepared and taste tested for sensory evaluation throughout the course and student grades will be based on participation in this course requirement.

title: Intermediate Nutrition
description: Intermediate approach toward understanding energy metabolism with an emphasis on mechanisms that regulate fuel intake, storage and utilization during normal and altered states of energy demands. Prerequisites: Grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 213 . Grade of C- or better in BIO 111 , BIO 277 or KIN 292 , CHE 103 , CHE 104 .

title: Diet and Disease
description: Principles of how nutrition plays a role in chronic disease development, prevention and treatment. Emphasis paid to analyzing data relevant to food availability, nutrient quality, and chronic disease incidence. Prerequisites: C- or higher in CHE 110 or CHE 112 . C- or higher in CHE 104 or CHE 114 ; C- or higher in BIO 277 / BIO 277L or KIN 292 / KIN 292L .

title: Cultural Foods and Global Nutrition
description: This course examines diet and food choices in the context of culture, religion, geography, and life experiences. Issues related to food insecurity and malnutrition globally are also explored. Prerequisites: NUTR major or minor. grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 213 and NTR 313 .

title: Community Nutrition
description: Current community nutrition trends with emphasis on community services, government projects, and grant proposal writing. Students will engage in community service work to gain experience with important community issues. Prerequisites: NUTR major or minor. grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 213 , NTR 302 , and NTR 313 ; or permission of instructor .

title: Management Practices for Dietetics
description: Management practices and administration within foodservice and clinical dietetics settings. Operational assessment, evaluation, and cost control related to foodservice systems in commercial and noncommercial settings. Prerequisites: Grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 309 and NTR 482 .

title: Undergraduate Research  2-6
description: Individual study. Prerequisites: GPA of 3.0 in nutrition, biology, and chemistry courses, AND permission of the instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit if topic changes.

title: Nutrition and Human Metabolism
description: Structure, function, and metabolism of nutrients and related compounds; integration of nutrient metabolism at the cellular level with total body function; practical application of basic principles of nutrient metabolism. Prerequisites: Minimum grade of C in NTR 320 or NTR 413 . minimum grade of C- in BIO 277 or KIN 292 , and CHE 103 (or CHE 111 ); CHE 104 (or CHE 114 ); CHE 110 (or CHE 112 ); CHE 205 and CHE 206 (or CHE 351 ); or their equivalents as determined by the instructor.

title: Nutrition Assessment
description: Assessment of nutritional status of healthy and ill persons before initiation of medical nutrition therapy. Prerequisites: Grade of C (2.0) or better in BIO 277 or KIN 292 . completion of NTR 213 , NTR 313 , and NTR 413 .

title: Child and Adolescent Nutrition
description: Nutritional needs of children and adolescents; methods of evaluating nutritional status of these groups; effects of nutrition on development. Prerequisites: Grade of C (2.0) or better in NTR 213 or equivalent, and BIO 277 or equivalent.

title: Advanced Nutrition
description: Biochemical and physiological aspects of nutrient metabolism and utilization. Nutrient metabolism, flux, and requirements during the lifecycle and during fed/fasted states will be discussed, with emphasis on mechanisms that regulate these processes. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in NTR 313 , NTR 413 , NTR 431 . Grade of C- or better in BIO 277 or KIN 292 , or equivalents as determined by the instructor.

title: Medical Nutrition Therapy
description: Clinical aspects of nutrition. Development and use of therapeutic diets to combat nutritional diseases and physiological disorders. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in NTR 313 , NTR 413 , NTR 431 , NTR 450 , NTR 460 , and BIO 277 or KIN 292 .

title: Medical Nutrition Therapy
description: Part 1 of 2-semester course sequence. Course focuses on applying Nutrition Care Process to determine weight-inclusive medical nutrition therapy for patients/clients experiencing physiological stress or disease using evidence-based guidelines. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ( NTR 320 or NTR 413 ), NTR 420 , NTR 431 . Corequisites: NTR 460 .

title: Medical Nutrition Therapy
description: Part 2 of a 2-semester course sequence. Course focuses on applying Nutrition Care Process and evidence-based guidelines to treat clients experiencing physiological stress or disease using weight-inclusive medical nutrition therapy. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in NTR 413 , NTR 431 , NTR 474 . Grade of C- or better in BIO 277 or KIN 292 .

title: Sports Nutrition
description: Application of sports nutrition recommendations among athletes. Principles of diet planning and goal setting for optimizing training and athletic performance. Common and current nutrition-related topics in sports nutrition. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in NTR 413 or equivalent required and instructor permission. C- or better in BIO 277 , CHE 103 , CHE 104 , CHE 110 or CHE 111 , CHE 112 , CHE 114 , CHE 115 .

title: Professionalism in Dietetics
description: Capstone course in dietetics covering professional practice guidelines, public policy, healthcare systems and/or policies, reimbursement, and a review of the five subject areas of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics and registration exam. Prerequisites: Minimum grade of C in NTR 282 , NTR 313 , NTR 413 , and NTR 431 . Minimum grade of C- in BIO 277 or KIN 292 ; Corequisites: NTR 460 .

title: Honors Work  3-6
description: Honors Work. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. 3.30 GPA in the major, 12 s.h. in the major; Notes: May be repeated for credit if the topic of study changes.

title: Field Experience in Nutrition  2-6
description: Supervised field experience in nutrition-related work sites. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in NTR 413 . Notes: May be repeated twice for up to 6 s.h. of credit.

title: Supervised Professional Experience  1-4
description: Supervised professional experience in selected commercial or industrial organizations, public or private agencies in accordance with the major course of study of the student.

title: Nutrition and Human Metabolism
description: Structure, function, and metabolism of nutrients and related compounds; integration of nutrient metabolism at the cellular level with total body function; practical application of basic principles of nutrient metabolism.

title: Child and Adolescent Nutrition
description: Nutritional needs of children and adolescents; methods of evaluating nutritional status of these groups; effects of nutrition on development.

title: Advanced Nutrition
description: Biochemical and physiological aspects of nutrient metabolism and utilization. Nutrient metabolism, flux, and requirements during the lifecycle and during fed/fasted states will be discussed, with emphasis on mechanisms that regulate these processes. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in NTR 531 or equivalent as determined by the instructor.

title: Medical Nutrition Therapy
description: Clinical aspects of nutrition. Development and use of therapeutic diets to combat nutritional diseases and physiological disorders. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in NTR 531 , NTR 550, and NTR 560 . Corequisites: NTR 573L .

title: Directed Study in Nutrition   1-6
description: Prerequisite: Permission of graduate faculty member; Notes: May be repeated for credit. Grade: Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U).

title: Supervised Preparation for Professional and Community Engagement
description: Preparation for clinical supervised practice experiences in dietetics; helping relationships skills, human resource management, marketing strategies for revenue generating services in dietetics, appropriate professional conduct in patient care. Prerequisites: Admission into the DI program or NTR 573 and NTR 560 .

title: Practicum in Clinical Dietetics   3-6
description: For Dietetic Internship students only. Practical experience in the professional areas of dietetics: management, clinical, and community. Prerequisites: Grade of B or better in NTR 693 . Notes: Required for Dietetic Internship students. Combination of credit not to exceed 9 credit hours.

title: Practicum in Clinical Dietetics   3-6
description: For Dietetic Internship students only. Practical experience in the professional areas of dietetics: management, clinical, and community. Prerequisites: Grade of B or better in NTR 693 . Notes: Required for Dietetic Internship students. Combination of credit not to exceed 9 credit hours.

title: Practicum in Clinical Dietetics   3-6
description: For Dietetic Internship students only. Practical experience in the professional areas of dietetics: management, clinical, and community. Prerequisites: Grade of B or better in NTR 693 . Notes: Required for Dietetic Internship students. Combination of credit not to exceed 9 credit hours.

title: Nutrition Education
description: Philosophy, principles, methods, and materials involved in nutrition education. Emphasis on development of nutrition education curriculum and programs in school and community. Prerequisites: NTR 550 or equivalent or permission of instructor.

title: Seminar in Nutrition
description: Review, analysis and presentation of recent research findings and issues in food and nutrition. Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic varies. Enrollment for two credits requires presentation of seminar; Grade: NTR 609A , 1 hour credit, graded Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U); NTR 609B , 2 hours credit, graded by letter grade.

title: Seminar in Nutrition
description: Review, analysis and presentation of recent research findings and issues in food and nutrition. Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic varies. Enrollment for two credits requires presentation of seminar; Grade: NTR 609A , 1 hour credit, graded Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U); NTR 609B , 2 hours credit, graded by letter grade.

title: Current Trends in Nutrition
description: Emphasis on current trends in nutrition research/education from a molecular, cellular and/or human perspective. Prerequisites: Senior or graduate level course in nutrition or permission of instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit when topic varies.

title: Supervised Preparation for Culinary Medicine
description: Practical experience to combine a comprehensive knowledge of nutrition and dietetics, culinary medicine, and the art of food and cooking to prepare satisfying and delicious meals that are consistent with real-world budgets, time constraints and better overall physical and mental health. Corequisites: Must be enrolled in the Dietetic Internship, Post-Baccalaureate Certificate program.

title: Gene Expression and Protein Metabolism
description: Integration of cell biology and protein metabolism in relation to gene expression and regulation of enzyme activity in mammalian cells. Critical analysis of recent literature. Prerequisites: Previous course in general nutrition, biochemistry and mammalian physiology and unconditional admission to the graduate program in Nutrition or other life sciences, or permission of instructor.

title: Energy, Carbohydrate, Lipid Metabolism
description: Analysis of energy intake and metabolism; carbohydrate and lipid absorption, transport, and tissue-specific utilization. Clinical applications. Critical analysis of recent literature. Prerequisites: NTR 625 , previous course in general nutrition, biochemistry and mammalian physiology, and unconditional admission to the graduate program in Nutrition or other life sciences. or permission of instructor.

title: Antioxidants and Bioactive Food Components
description: Metabolism and function of selected bioactive food components, such as flavonoids, stanols, and sterols, anthocyanins, carotenoids, polyphenolics, indole-3-carbinol. Clinical applications. Critical analysis of recent literature. Prerequisites: NTR 625 , previous course in general nutrition, biochemistry, and mammalian physiology, and unconditional admission to the graduate program in Nutrition or other life sciences. or permission of instructor.

title: Vitamins and Minerals
description: Metabolism functions of selected vitamins. Regulation of selected trace metals emphasizing nutrient-gene interactions. Clinical applications. Critical analysis of recent literature. Prerequisites: NTR 625 , previous course in general nutrition, biochemistry and mammalian physiology, and unconditional admission to the graduate program in Nutrition or other life sciences. or permission of instructor.

title: Teaching Practicum in Nutrition
description: Provides a supervised, structured learning experience in teaching for graduate students in the Department of Nutrition. Professors provide guidance for graduate students during the experience. Prerequisites: Admission to the graduate program in nutrition or permission of instructor.

title: Problems in Food and Nutrition  2-4
description: Individual student problems related to food and nutrition. Prerequisites: Permission of instructor. Notes: May be repeated for credit. Grade: Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U).

title: Research Skill Development  2-6
description: Notes: May be repeated for credit; Grade: Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U).

title: Nutrition Research Methodology
description: Diverse research techniques used in cellular, small animal, and human experimental studies in nutritional sciences. Orientation to research methodologies, grant proposal writing, ethical use of human (IRB) and animal (IACUC) models, and policy in nutrition. Prerequisites: NTR 531 or equivalent as determined by the department.

title: Medical Nutrition Therapy
description: Part 1 of 2-semester course sequence. Course focuses on applying Nutrition Care Process to determine weight-inclusive medical nutrition therapy for patients/clients experiencing physiological stress or disease using evidence-based guidelines.

title: Medical Nutrition Therapy
description: Part 2 of a 2-semester course sequence. Course focuses on applying Nutrition Care Process and evidence-based guidelines to treat clients experiencing physiological stress or disease using weight-inclusive medical nutrition therapy. Prerequisites: NTR 674 .

title: Nutrition and Physical Performance
description: Effects of nutrition on physical performance, alterations in nutrient metabolism from increased activity, interaction of diet and exercise on aging and disease processes, training and competition diets examined. Prerequisites: Course work in biochemistry and physiology required, 531, 560, or equivalent courses, BIO 277 .

title: Supervised Preparation for Practicum in Clinical Dietetics
description: Development of nutritional therapies for certain disease conditions. Evaluation of the physiological and biochemical bases for such therapies. Prerequisites: Admission to DI program or NTR 573 and permission of instructor.

title: Thesis  1-6
description: Notes: Required of all candidates for the Master of Science degree; Credit may be divided over two or more semesters; Grade: Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U).

title: Directed Study in Nutrition  1-6
description: Prerequisite: Permission of graduate faculty member; Notes: May be repeated for credit, grading method is Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory (S/U).
